title: 4NF Decomposition Algorithm
description: Algorithm to decompose a relation into Fourth Normal Form
code:
ALGORITHM DecomposeTo4NF(R, F, MVDs)
BEGIN
 RESULT ← {R}
 WHILE exists R_i in RESULT with non-trivial MVD A →→ B DO
 Decompose R_i into:
 R_i1 ← (A ∪ B)
 R_i2 ← (R_i - (B - A))
 RESULT ← (RESULT - {R_i}) ∪ {R_i1, R_i2}
 F ← recompute FDs from RESULT
 MVDs ← recompute MVDs from RESULT
 END WHILE
 RETURN RESULT
END
explanation: This algorithm takes a relation R with its functional dependencies F and multivalued dependencies MVDs, and iteratively decomposes it until all relations are in 4NF. For each non-trivial MVD A→→B, it creates two new relations: one containing A and B, and another containing A with the remaining attributes.

complexity.time: O(n²) worst case with n attributes

title: Check if MVD is Trivial
description: Function to determine if a multivalued dependency is trivial
code:
FUNCTION IsTrivialMVD(R, A, B)
BEGIN
 // Check if B ⊆ A
 IF B ⊆ A THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 
 // Check if A ∪ B = all attributes of R
 unionAB ← A ∪ B
 IF unionAB = Attributes(R) THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 
 // Otherwise non-trivial
 RETURN FALSE
END
explanation: This function checks two conditions to determine if MVD A→→B is trivial: (1) if B is a subset of A, or (2) if the union of A and B contains all attributes of the relation. If either condition is true, the MVD is trivial and does not cause redundancy.

complexity.time: O(n) where n is number of attributes

title: Lossless-Join Verification for MVD Decomposition
description: Algorithm to verify if decomposition preserves lossless-join property
explanation: This function verifies that when decomposing a relation into two parts, the join operation will not create spurious tuples. It checks if the common attributes functionally determine either of the decomposed relations, which guarantees lossless-join.
